steps.title: Spot the contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts the typical behavior of many female wood ducks (visiting dozens of nests and laying eggs for others to incubate) with Subject 7F64B’s actions (visiting only a few nests and incubating her own eggs).

steps.title: Pick the contrasting transition
steps.content:
The word “alternatively” signals a contrasting option, showing that Subject 7F64B behaves differently from the pattern described earlier.

The other three choices either add an example (“for example,”), specify a member of the group (“in particular,”), or imply similarity (“similarly,”), none of which convey the needed contrast.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
